channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Buddhist hall housing ‘eternal flame’ burns down in Japan
item.description: Buddhist hall housing ‘eternal flame’ burns down in Japan

item.title: Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited
item.description: Israeli officials and media indicate renewed conflict with Iran is being actively considered despite a shaky deadlock.

item.title: DR Congo cancel World Cup training camp in Kinshasa over Ebola outbreak
item.description: DRC's public sendoff in the capital was also cancelled before their departure for the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Taiwan’s President Lai says he would be happy to speak with Donald Trump
item.description: US and Taiwan leaders have not spoken since Washington shifted diplomatic recognition from Taipei to Beijing in 1979.

item.title: ‘King’ Emery hailed as Aston Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Europa League
item.description: Villa dominated Freiburg in the final in Istanbul to win their first trophy since 1996 and European title since 1982.

item.title: Walid Daqqa: A Parallel Life
item.description: Walid Daqqa, the Palestinian prisoner who turned decades in Israeli jails into moments of thought, creativity and love.

item.title: The numbers behind global mental health and its different disorders
item.description: Nearly one in eight people in the world lives with a mental disorder, and one person dies by suicide every 43 seconds.

item.title: Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque illuminated in Israeli flag colours
item.description: Palestinian officials condemned Israel’s illumination of Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque with Israeli flag colours and slogans.

item.title: Iran war day 83: Tehran ‘reviewing’ latest US response to end conflict
item.description: Iran says diplomacy with the US remains open as Hormuz tensions deepen and outrage grows over Israel’s flotilla arrests.

item.title: South Korea’s stock market soars as Samsung union calls off planned strike
item.description: Benchmark KOSPI rises more than 8 percent following deal to avert strike at top memory chip maker.

item.title: MVP Gilgeous-Alexander stars as Thunder level series with Spurs
item.description: Defending champs OKC win 122-113 as Gilgeous-Alexander scores 30 points and provides 9 assists, overshadowing Wembanyama.

item.title: UN adopts resolution supporting international court’s climate ruling
item.description: Some 141 UN member states voted in support of the ICJ's finding climate change is an 'existential threat'.

item.title: Bolivia’s president reshuffles cabinet amid anti-government protests
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz has announced a cabinet reshuffle and other measures as protests escalate.

item.title: Argentina protesters condemn Milei healthcare funding cuts
item.description: Hundreds marched in Buenos Aires against President Javier Milei’s austerity policies and cuts to Argentina’s healthcare

item.title: Cubans react to US indictment of former President Raul Castro
item.description: Cubans in Havana and Miami react to the US charges against former Cuban President Raul Castro.

item.title: Nvidia posts record profit of $58.3bn amid AI chip boom
item.description: Chip giant announces $80bn stock buyback scheme and dividend hike in boon to shareholders.

item.title: Indonesians sue government over deadly Sumatra flood response
item.description: Survivors of Indonesia’s deadly Sumatra floods are suing the government over inadequate disaster recovery

item.title: US lifts sanctions on Francesca Albanese, UN expert on Palestinian rights
item.description: Albanese is among several people sanctioned by the US for advocacy against Israel's genocidal war in Gaza.

item.title: Trump says no escalation expected with Cuba after Castro indictment
item.description: President Trump said the United States is “freeing up Cuba” and ruled out escalation after the US indicted Castro

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran says it is reviewing US response to proposal
item.description: Israeli attacks have killed 3,073 people across Lebanon since March, according to the Lebanese Health Ministry.

item.title: US police officers sue Trump over $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponisation’ fund
item.description: Officers Harry Dunn and Daniel Hodges say the fund will likely be used to 'reward' rioters who attacked the US Capitol.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX unveils filing for blockbuster IPO
item.description: A successful sale could value the company at a record-setting $1.75 trillion.

item.title: US condemns Israel’s Ben-Gvir while sanctioning Gaza flotilla organisers
item.description: Mike Huckabee says Ben-Gvir 'betrayed dignity' in video taunting flotilla activists, day after US sanctioned organisers.

item.title: US lawyers say man on death row could be executed with expired lethal drugs
item.description: Execution method has long raised questions about cruel and unusual punishment and botched executions.

item.title: Bolivian president to reshuffle cabinet amid antigovernment protests
item.description: Rodrigo Paz is under pressure from weeks of demonstrations and poor economic conditions.
